# Meeting Summary for Working Group Meeting

**NOTICE**: This summary was auto-generated by Zoom's "AI". AI-generated
content may be inaccurate or misleading. Always check for accuracy. If in

https://youtube.com/@GraphQLFoundation/playlists

- Meeting start: 2026-07-02T17:27:56Z
- Meeting end: 2026-07-02T19:10:17Z
- Summary start: 2026-07-02T17:27:56Z
- Summary end: 2026-07-02T19:04:57Z

The GraphQL Working Group held their July meeting to review multiple specification proposals and updates. The group discussed best practices for writing robust applications, with Martin presenting a new documentation page on handling schema changes that received approval for immediate merging. James updated the team on one-of-inhabitability specifications, which moved to Stage 2 pending final review, while Pascal provided an overview of OTel semantic conventions requiring TSC approval before merging. Benjie presented several proposals including sibling errors, empty selection sets, empty object types, interfaces with no fields, and unions with no members, with the group supporting these changes and moving them to RFC 2 status. The team also reviewed the GraphQL over HTTP specification, which was nearly ready for release with only minor editorial changes remaining. The conversation ended with plans to complete spec freeze preparations for the upcoming August meeting and September/October specification cut.

## Next Steps

- mark: Update the agenda file by deleting one copy of Martine's name and adding himself.
- Martin: Add an admonition about future added values to the "writing robust applications" best practices page.
- Pascal: Create a summary of the opinionated decisions made in the OTel semantic conventions PR, highlighting areas for TSC review and feedback.
- Pascal: Investigate and decide on the correct naming convention (server vs. service) for the OTel spec, comparing with HTTP and gRPC examples.
- Benjie: Implement the "empty selection sets" change in GraphQL JS.
- Benjie: Update the "empty object types" proposal to require curly braces for empty object type definitions.
- Benjie: Proceed with the proposals for "empty interfaces", "empty unions", and "empty enums" as separate RFCs, targeting RFC1 stage.
- Benjie: Finalize and merge the GraphQL over HTTP specification PR #404 once Michael gives approval.
- leebyron: Update the main GraphQL spec to reference the GraphQL over HTTP spec once it's released.
- All TSC members: Review the OTel semantic conventions PR summary and provide feedback or approval for merging.

## Summary

### GraphQL Working Group Meeting

The GraphQL Working Group meeting began with introductions from attendees including Lee, Benjie from Graphile, James from Airbnb, Martin, Pascal from Chilik, Michael from Telegram, Matt from Meta, and Mark from Yelp. Lee announced that the group would conduct a spec freeze before next month's meeting, aiming to complete it by August for the September/October cut. The meeting then moved to reviewing the agenda, which included discussing best practices for writing robust applications, including topics about sibling errors, empty selection sets, interfaces, and schema validation requirements.

### GraphQL Schema Change Guidance Discussion

The team discussed reordering agenda items, with Pascal requesting to move his item higher and Benjie suggesting to prioritize one-off items. Martin presented a proposal for adding guidance on writing robust applications to handle schema changes, particularly for new enum values and union types, which the team agreed to merge quickly and iterate on later. James then discussed a PR regarding one-of inhabitability in the GraphQL spec, explaining that it addresses issues with infinite values and recursive input types, with the implementation already in place in GraphQL JS.

### Input Specification Review Progress

The team discussed whether to allow empty values in input types, with James and Leebyron agreeing that existing rules around input objects already handle null values sufficiently. They reviewed a specification that is ready to move from stage 2 to stage 3, with Benjie raising a minor concern about potential confusion regarding input object types but deciding to leave the text as-is. Pascal provided an update on OTEL, mentioning he addressed comments and implemented opinionated choices where decisions were needed, with the specification now ready for merge pending feedback from Apollo team members.

### Semantic Convention Specification Merge Discussion

Pascal discussed moving forward with merging a specification into the Semantic Convention repository, seeking approval from the TSC. Leebyron suggested creating a summary of key decisions and proposed conducting a TSC vote to ensure all members have an opportunity to review and provide feedback. Mark raised concerns about the complexity of reviewing the large specification and suggested implementing a draft mode label to allow for easier updates post-merge. The team debated the use of "server" versus "service" in naming conventions, with Pascal agreeing to research examples in HTTP and gRPC specifications to determine the appropriate terminology.

### GraphQL Specification Updates Discussion

Benjie presented updates on two GraphQL specification changes. The first addresses an issue in GraphQL JS where errors arrays might grow after receiving a response, which has been resolved in version 17 by changing the behavior from a "must" to a "should" requirement. The second proposal allows empty selection sets, which will help with client-derived fields and schema design, though it requires implementation in GraphQL JS. Leebyron marked both proposals as RFC 2 pending implementation, and the group discussed that while this makes the grammar more general, it should not break existing documents but might not be compatible with older servers.

### GraphQL Empty Types Discussion

The team discussed allowing empty object types and partial schemas in GraphQL. Leebyron and Benjie proposed adding curly braces to make it clear when defining a type with no fields, while Pascal suggested allowing empty input objects and enums. Matt highlighted the need for clarity in schema evolution and deprecation processes. Benjie emphasized the value of this feature for both LLMs and human users, particularly in mutation payloads and training courses. The group agreed that allowing empty types would improve schema management and validation, with Benjie taking responsibility for updating the GraphQL syntax to support this change.

### GraphQL Schema and Specification Updates

The team discussed several proposals related to empty schema definitions, including enums, unions, and interfaces. They agreed to merge these into RFC1, focusing on ensuring that schemas remain valid after removing deprecated elements. The group also approved the GraphQL over HTTP specification, which will be released in September 2026, while the query method in HTTP is planned for GraphQL 1.1. The team decided to proceed with the current specification release without waiting for the query method, as it represents a significant simplification from previous proposals.
